differential_diagnoses: Hypervirulent Klebsiella pneumoniae (hvKP) syndrome with metastatic infection
Infective endocarditis with systemic and ocular septic emboli
Disseminated fungal infection (e.g., Candida species or invasive mold)
Metastatic malignancy (e.g., lung adenocarcinoma with hepatic and ocular metastases)
next_steps: Perform diagnostic vitrectomy or vitreous tap for Gram stain, culture, and fungal studies
Perform image-guided biopsy of the hepatic mass
Repeat blood cultures (aerobic and anaerobic) and obtain fungal/mycobacterial cultures
Consult ophthalmology for urgent evaluation, management of endophthalmitis, and intraocular pressure control
Evaluate for specific hvKP virulence factors (e.g., mucoviscosity testing, rmpA gene PCR)
Serial neurologic examinations and monitoring for intracranial complications
Arrange for Infectious Disease and Ophthalmology subspecialty follow-up

differential_diagnoses: Metastatic lung malignancy with secondary bacterial abscesses
next_steps: Perform diagnostic vitrectomy or vitreous tap for Gram stain, culture, and fungal studies
Obtain HbA1c and fingerstick glucose to assess for occult diabetes mellitus
Perform image-guided aspiration/drainage of the hepatic abscess for microbiology
Consult Ophthalmology for urgent surgical intervention and intravitreal antibiotics
Continue broad-spectrum empiric antibiotics and monitor response
Perform molecular testing (e.g., PCR for rmpA or specific virulence factors) on blood or abscess cultures
Consult Infectious Disease for consideration of specific pathogen-targeted therapy once cultures return
